Investor Anatomy
US Liquidity and Monetary Conditions
Home
US Liquidity and Monetary Conditions
Synthesises Federal Reserve liquidity measures, money supply, real-rate signals and SOFR positioning to assess the current US monetary and liquidity regime.

Regime Assessment:

  • Regime: Transitional / Fragile Balance
  • Regime Confidence Index: Medium Confidence
  • The regime is fragile.

Why This Regime:

  • The primary driver is the M2 Money Supply signal, which holds high aggregation weight and high confidence, currently indicating a Neutral regime (1).
  • Speculative positioning across the SOFR curve reinforces a non-stretched, "Normal" state with balanced biases (2)(3).
  • The Federal Reserve Liquidity Composite was downweighted because it carries mixed interpretation confidence and an internal conflict flag, despite its expansionary reading (4).
  • The Real Interest Rate Trend signal was further downweighted due to low confidence and internal data inconsistencies (5).

Alignment & Tensions:

  • High-confidence signals for M2 growth and SOFR positioning align on a mid-cycle period of stability (1)(2).
  • Tension exists between expansionary liquidity flows from reverse repo (RRP) declines (4) and restrictive (Bearish) real 10-year yields (5).
  • These tensions do not overturn the regime call as the dominant high-weight indicators point toward a neutral, transitional state rather than sustained expansion or stress.

Scenario Balance:

  • Dominant scenario: Continued stability in broad money supply with range-biased interest rate expectations.
  • Primary upside risk: A re-acceleration of M2 momentum above 1.0% and broader alignment of bank reserves (1).
  • Primary downside risk: A sharp contraction in Federal Reserve assets or a reversal in RRP usage (4).

What Would Change the Regime:

  • A reversal in ON RRP usage or a sharp contraction in M2 growth below 2.0% (4)(1).
  • Real 10-year yields rising significantly above their 6-month moving average trend (5).
  • Speculative positioning in SOFR futures moving into extreme crowding-risk territory (z-score > 2.0) (2).
Inputs
Federal Reserve Liquidity Composite Signal

Signal Summary:

  • The Federal Reserve Liquidity Composite currently indicates an Expansion regime, driven by a composite z-score of 1.834179 as of 2026-02-28 (1). This represents a transition from earlier Neutral states to sustained expansion.
  • Conviction Band: Medium | Interpretation Confidence: Mixed Signals | Internal Conflict Flag: Yes.

Key Dynamics:

  • The composite's expansion is primarily supported by significant easing from overnight reverse repo (RRP) usage, with YoY and 3M changes at -98.67% and -58.85% respectively, indicating a strong Bullish signal (1).
  • Fed Total Assets (WALCL) show Bullish momentum with a 3M change of +0.816%, despite a -2.367% YoY contraction (1).
  • Conversely, Reserve Balances (WRESBAL) remain Bearish, contracting by -12.08% YoY (1). M2 Money Supply is Neutral, with +4.60% YoY and +0.89% 3M momentum (as of 2025-12-31) (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A significant reversal in ON RRP usage, or a sharper contraction in Fed Assets and M2, would challenge the Expansion regime.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Continued liquidity expansion, supported by the ongoing sharp decline in RRP usage.
  • Upside secondary: Broader alignment of M2 and Reserve balances towards easing, reinforcing the Expansion regime.
  • Downside residual: A renewed tightening trend in Fed Assets or a halt in RRP declines, leading to a shift back to Neutral or Contraction.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months) – The signal is based on monthly data and 3-month/YoY changes, reflecting medium-term liquidity trends (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium – The strong composite value is noteworthy, but mixed underlying components warrant balanced consideration in broader macro aggregates.

Macro Relevance:

  • Macro dimension informed: Liquidity. This signal gauges the overall monetary conditions and credit availability in the US economy (1).
  • Cycle position: Mid-cycle, as liquidity remains generally accommodative despite some tightening pressures.
  • Typical interaction with other macro signals: Often correlates with risk assets, credit spreads (1), and inflation expectations (1); strong liquidity typically supports growth signals.

Data & References:

  • M2 Money Stock (M2SL): Latest value for M2_YoY 4.60% as of 2025-12-31 (1).
  • Federal Reserve Total Assets (WALCL): Latest FED_YoY -2.36% as of 2026-02-28 (1).
  • Reserve Balances with Federal Reserve Banks (WRESBAL): Latest RES_YoY -12.08% as of 2026-02-28 (1).
  • ON Reverse Repo usage (RRPONTSYD): Latest RRP_YoY -98.67% as of 2026-02-28 (1).
  • Additional public datasets: SOFR positioning data could enhance the understanding of short-term funding market dynamics and complement the liquidity composite.
M2 Money Supply Signal

Signal Summary:

  • The M2 Money Supply signal is currently Neutral as of 2025-12-01, indicating stable monetary expansion (1). The signal transitioned from Bullish in prior months back to a Neutral state (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium | Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence | Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Key Dynamics:

  • The latest M2 Year-over-Year growth is 4.60% and the 3-month change is 0.90% as of 2025-12-01 (1).
  • The signal is Neutral because YoY growth is between the 2.0% and 6.0% thresholds, and 3-month momentum is between -1.0% and 1.0% (1).
  • Momentum has slowed from recent Bullish readings, with 3-month momentum just below the 1.0% acceleration threshold (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A sustained decline in M2 Year-over-Year growth below 2.0% or 3-month momentum below -1.0% would reverse the signal to Bearish (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: M2 growth maintains its Neutral regime, with moderate YoY growth and 3-month momentum (1).
  • Upside secondary: A slight re-acceleration in 3-month momentum above 1.0% would quickly shift the signal to Bullish (1).
  • Downside residual: A notable deceleration causing YoY growth below 2.0% or 3-month momentum below -1.0% would trigger a Bearish signal (1).

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months). The signal relies on 12-month and 3-month growth rates, reflecting medium-term liquidity trends (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: High. It provides a clear, rules-based assessment of monetary conditions, making it a critical input for liquidity composites (1).

Macro Relevance:

  • This signal primarily informs on liquidity and monetary conditions (1).
  • It is currently indicative of a mid-cycle expansion, where liquidity growth is stable and supportive, but not exceptionally strong.
  • M2 growth typically interacts with inflation expectations (2), real interest rates (3), and overall credit conditions (4), influencing asset classes sensitive to monetary dynamics (1).

Data & References:

  • M2 Money Stock (M2SL) from FRED, latest observation 2025-12-01 (1).
  • The 12-month Year-over-Year growth of 4.60% and 3-month momentum of 0.90% for 2025-12-01 are the most influential data points (1).
  • Federal Reserve Liquidity Composite (5), Explicit Neutral Rate (r*) Proxy Signal (6).
Real Interest Rate Trend Signal

Signal Summary:

  • The US 10-Year Real Yield signal, as per the provided table, is currently in a "Bearish" regime as of 2026-02-01 (1). This indicates a period of restrictive real monetary conditions.
  • However, there is an internal conflict as the explicit methodology would classify the latest value as "Neutral" (1).
  • Conviction Band: Low | Interpretation Confidence: Low Confidence | Internal Conflict Flag: Yes.

Key Dynamics:

  • The latest 10-year real yield (DFII10) is 1.85% (2026-02-01) (1).
  • Its 6-month moving average trend (DFII10_Trend) is 1.831667% (2026-02-01) (1).
  • The signal's shift from "Neutral" to "Bearish" (2026-01-01) suggests a potential move towards tighter conditions (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: The real yield consistently falls more than 0.05 percentage points below its 6-month moving average, signaling accommodative conditions (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant; risks balanced. The conflicting signal interpretation introduces considerable uncertainty.
  • Most plausible upside risk (accommodative shift): The real yield drops to below 1.78% (more than 0.05% below its trend), moving the signal to "Bullish" (1).
  • Most plausible downside risk (more restrictive shift): The real yield rises further above its trend (>1.88%), solidifying "Bearish" conditions (1).

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months), reflecting the 6-month moving average used for trend identification (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Low, due to the low confidence stemming from the internal data inconsistency.

Macro Relevance:

  • This signal primarily informs monetary conditions and liquidity dynamics.
  • It serves as a diagnostic tool for assessing the restrictiveness of real rates within the economic cycle.
  • It often interacts with inflation expectations and bond market sentiment, influencing equity-bond correlations (1).

Data & References:

  • FRED DFII10 (10-Year Treasury Inflation-Indexed Security, Real Yield), latest value 1.85% (2026-02-01) (1).
  • Datapoints most influential are Value_DFII10 and DFII10_Trend for 2026-02-01 (1).
  • Additional public datasets that would improve depth or reliability include the 5-year TIPS real yield and market-implied inflation breakevens.
SOFR 3M Individual Market Analysis

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Spec_Net_%OI of -4.0 and a Spec_zscore of 0.25, this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Data uncertainty, not Trend continuation.
  • The signal currently resides in a Normal synthetic state characterized by a Balanced_Short_Bias, reflecting a lack of positioning stretch (1).
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Stretch rules define Spec_zscore values between -1 and +1 as a normal, un-stretched regime (1).
  • Participant interpretation treats managed money positioning as the primary gauge of policy-path conviction and repricing risk (1).
  • Participant roles distinguish between speculative directional bets and commercial balance-sheet hedging (1).
  • SOFR-3M - CHICAGO MERCANTILE EXCHANGE: Latest observation dated 2026-02-03.

Key Dynamics:

  • A Spec_zscore of 0.25 indicates that speculative traders are not currently crowded, leaving ample room for participation in either direction (1).
  • The Flow_4w of -1.3 is classified as a "Long_Reduction," suggesting a mild fading of conviction in the prevailing higher-for-longer narrative (1).
  • Positioning tension remains low (0.48), as the market structure is balanced with no immediate squeeze or exhaustion risks (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: The regime would transition to a Crowding-risk state if the Spec_zscore exceeds ±2.0 (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant; risks balanced due to un-stretched positioning.
  • Upside risk: A dovish shift in FOMC communication triggering a rapid reduction of net short positions.
  • Downside risk: A hawkish data surprise (CPI/NFP) forcing a move toward extreme short crowding.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks) as CoT data focuses on near-term policy-path pricing shifts (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium, as it provides a critical sanity check on interest rate consensus (2).

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the liquidity and pricing dimension by measuring the conviction behind expected Fed policy adjustments (2).
  • Positioned in the mid-to-late cycle where policy sensitivity to data surprises is high (3).
  • Typically interacts with Fed Tone signals to identify misalignments between market pricing and official guidance (4).

Data & References:

  • SOFR-3M Individual Market Analysis (CoT): 2026-02-03 (1).
  • Key influencing metrics: Spec_zscore (0.25) and Spec_Net_%OI (-4.0).
  • Federal Reserve Tone Decoder and US Macro Regime State provide essential narrative context (4)(3).
SOFR 5Y Individual Market Analysis

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Spec Net %OI of 17.6% and a Spec z-score of 0.798, this setup aligns with Transition-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Regime shift, not trend continuation.
  • The signal is currently in a Normal regime characterized by a balanced long bias and fading speculative conviction (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Positioning stretch is governed by Spec_zscore, where |z| < 1 is classified as un-stretched or normal (1).
  • Flow dynamics (Flow_4w) opposing the net direction indicate conviction fading or early turn risk (1).
  • Participant interpretation weights managed money as an expression of strategic neutral-rate and medium-term macro beliefs (1).
  • Latest observation date: 2026-02-03.

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the deceleration in speculative length, with a 4-week flow of -3.4% opposing the net long stance (1).
  • Stabilisation is evident as the Spec_zscore has moderated from previous highs toward a neutral 0.798 level.
  • Internal tension is low, though the Long Reduction label suggests a strategic de-risking of medium-term rate bets (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A Spec_zscore exceeding ±2.0, indicating a shift into a crowding-risk regime.

Scenario Balance:

  • Dominant base case: Consolidation of medium-term rate expectations as positioning aligns with neutral-rate consensus.
  • Upside risk: A sudden shift toward a lower neutral-rate narrative triggering a renewed speculative long build.
  • Downside risk: Reflationary data forcing a liquidation of the remaining 17.6% speculative net long length.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months), as ERIS SOFR reflects strategic macro views rather than tactical meeting noise (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium, providing a reliable gauge of policy transmission without extreme tactical signals.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the pricing of medium-term policy transmission and neutral-rate (r*) expectations.
  • Cycle position: Mid-to-late cycle, sensitive to shifts in the growth-inflation balance.
  • Typically interacts with yield-curve slope signals and US inflation regime assessments.

Data & References:

  • 5 YEAR ERIS SOFR SWAP CoT Diagnostics: 2026-02-03 (1).
  • Most influential datapoints: Spec_zscore (0.798) and Flow_4w (-3.4).
  • Public datasets: 5-Year Treasury yields and OIS-SOFR swap spreads.
Investor Anatomy • US Liquidity and Monetary Conditions • Not Investment Advice
The information presented is for general informational purposes only and does not constitute financial or investment advice. It has been prepared without regard to individual objectives, financial situation, or needs. You should consider whether it is appropriate for your circumstances and seek independent advice where necessary.
